test_cases.prompt: let's discuss one of the pending ideas from the ai-for-ux-guide resource
test_cases.expected_output: Presents the to-discuss, target_type=literature seeds rows for that resource, lets the user pick one, then runs a real back-and-forth discussion (not a one-shot proposal) grounded in the source, gates on explicit confirmation, and writes a literature note holding only the confirmed Claim.

assertions.query: SELECT status FROM seeds WHERE slug LIKE '%'

test_cases.prompt: I confirm, that's the claim. Actually here's what I personally think about it too.
test_cases.expected_output: Accepts the confirmed claim, but flags the personal-opinion drift rather than folding it into the literature note — offers to log it as a new open question / seeds row instead.
